steps.title: Match the variable coefficients
steps.content:
A linear equation has no solution when the variable coefficients match but the constants do not. Expand the left side:
$$2(kx-n)=2kx-2n$$
So the $x$-coefficient on the left is $2k$.

steps.title: Solve for k
steps.content:
Match the $x$-coefficient on the left to the $x$-coefficient on the right:
$$2k=-\frac{22}{13}$$
$$k=-\frac{11}{13}$$

steps.title: Check the contradiction
steps.content: With this value, the $x$-terms cancel. Since $n>1$, the constant $-2n$ cannot equal $-\frac{34}{23}$, so the equation becomes a contradiction. The answer is -11/13.
